refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the operational context ensures clarity and testability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the agreed evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link uptime to support teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Monitor and report link availability status to stakeholders.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network communication paths with failover.
DP2: Use real-time link health monitoring and automatic reconnection protocols.
DP3: Provide link availability reporting and alerting mechanisms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network communication paths with failover.

coupling_check.notes: Redundancy and failover logic is logically separable from other system functions, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Risk of increased system complexity impacting maintainability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent decomposition levels for comprehensive link availability management.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation communication and control link management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send link status and health data to monitoring systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad system scope; contains teleoperation but also other functions, leading to moderate diffusion.
Vehicle module has minimal direct responsibility for link availability.
Primary module for teleoperation link management; highest containment and lowest diffusion.
Safety Services may monitor link status for safety alerts but not responsible for link availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability management within the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and clarifies ownership.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on network infrastructure outside Teleoperation module.
Risk of insufficient integration with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish clear interface contracts with Safety Services for safety-related link status notifications.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear mapping and minimal coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if link monitoring is duplicated outside Teleoperation.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment for the DP, supporting clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and network link monitoring is feasible within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of FR to Teleoperation IM is clear and aligns with stakeholder responsibilities.

recommended_revisions: Plan integration points with Safety Services for safety alerts related to link availability.
Consider further decomposition of FR2 and FR3 for detailed DP and IM assignments.

decision.justification: The current decomposition is sound but incomplete; further refinement of sub-FRs and inclusion of monitoring and reporting DPs is needed to fully address the FR.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link uptime to support teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Monitor and report link availability status to stakeholders.

revise.why: To explicitly capture all aspects of link availability including detection, recovery, and reporting for comprehensive coverage.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Teleoperation module can interface with Safety Services for safety alerts.
open_risks: Integration complexity with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
Potential system complexity from redundancy mechanisms.
evidence_needed: Network link availability statistics during operational testing.
Verification of failover mechanisms and alerting functionality.
